Skill Test Date To be notified later

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 188 vacancies have been announced for the post of Operator (Chemical) Trainee. The vacancies are subject to variation based on business requirements. Reservation is applicable as per Government of India norms for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD categories.

S. No. Post Name Total Vacancies
1 Operator (Chemical) Trainee 188
Grand Total 188

Note: Reservation as per Government of India norms applicable for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD categories.

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the crucial date i.e. 01-02-2026. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. The educational qualification must be full time and regular course from a recognized University/Institution. Candidates in final year of Graduation/Post Graduation are not eligible to apply.

Educational Qualification (Any One of the Following):

Option Essential Qualification Details
Option 1 Full time & regular B.Sc. (Chemistry) Degree with Physics as one of the subjects + Passing of NCVT examination in Attendant Operator (Chemical Plant) Trade [AO(CP)]
Option 2 Full time & regular Three Years' Diploma in Chemical Engineering/Technology + Successful completion of 1 Year Training (BOAT) under Apprentice Act
Option 3 4 years' / 3½ years' Sandwich Diploma in Chemical Engineering/Technology (Full time & regular)

Minimum Percentage Requirement:

Category Minimum Percentage Required
General/EWS/OBC (NCL) Minimum 55% marks in last year / aggregate of last two semesters of qualifying examination
SC/ST Minimum 50% marks in last year / aggregate of last two semesters of qualifying examination

Important Notes:

  • Only full time and regular courses are acceptable. Distance/Correspondence/Part-time courses are not eligible
  • Candidates in final year of Graduation/Post Graduation are not eligible
  • Reserved category candidates must submit valid Caste/EWS/Disability certificate in prescribed proforma

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limit is calculated as on the crucial date i.e. 01-02-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories.

Category Upper Age Limit (as on 01-02-2026)
Unreserved (General/EWS) 30 years
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 33 years
SC/ST 35 years
Ex-Serviceman / Children / Family members of victims of 1984 riots Additional 5 years concession

Note: Age relaxation as per Government of India guidelines. Category once submitted cannot be changed.

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Salary & Allowances

Selected candidates will undergo 6 months training and will receive consolidated stipend during training period. After successful completion of training, candidates will be absorbed in Grade II-(A6) with attractive salary and benefits.

During 6-Month Training Period:

Component Details
Consolidated Stipend Rs. 22,000/- per month
Hostel Accommodation Free of cost (on sharing basis during training)
Medical Facility For self at RCF Hospital
Sports Complex Free access to Sports Complex facility

After Successful Completion of Training (Regular Employment):

Component Details / Amount
Grade & Pay Scale Grade II-(A6): Rs. 22,000 - 60,000
Basic Pay (at minimum of scale) Rs. 22,000/-
VDA (Variable Dearness Allowance) 53.4% of Basic Pay
Perks 34% of Basic Pay
HRA (House Rent Allowance) 30% for Class A cities (subject to company accommodation availability)
Total Monthly Gross Salary (approx.) Rs. 47,800/- at minimum of scale

Additional Benefits:

  • Company accommodation (subject to availability) in lieu of HRA
  • Performance Related Pay (PRP)
  • Free Medical facility for self & dependents
  • Gratuity
  • Contributory Provident Fund (CPF)
  • Accident Insurance
  • Social Security Schemes as applicable

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee (as applicable). The fee must be paid online only. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

Category Application Fee
General/EWS/OBC (NCL) Rs. 700/-
SC/ST/Female/Ex-Serviceman candidates NIL (Exempted)

RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 consists of Computer Based Online Test, Document Scrutiny, Skill Test (Qualifying nature), and Pre-Employment Medical Examination. Final selection is based on marks obtained in Online Test only (100% weightage). Skill Test is qualifying in nature.

Computer Based Online Objective Type Test

Particulars Details
Total Number of Questions 100 Questions
Total Marks 200 Marks
Duration 90 Minutes
Type of Questions Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions
Negative Marking Yes (as per IBPS pattern)
Subjects Covered Technical Knowledge, General Awareness, Reasoning, Numerical Ability, English Language etc.

Document Scrutiny:

  • Provisionally shortlisted candidates will be called for Document Scrutiny in the ratio of 1:7 (Vacancy : Candidates)
  • All original documents along with self-attested copies must be produced

Skill Test (Qualifying Nature):

Particulars Details
Total Marks 100 Marks
Minimum Qualifying Marks (General/EWS/OBC-NCL) 60 Marks
Minimum Qualifying Marks (SC/ST) 55 Marks
Nature Qualifying only (marks not counted for final selection)

Final Selection:

  • Final selection is based on marks obtained in Computer Based Online Test only
  • 100% weightage is given to Online Test marks
  • Skill Test is qualifying in nature only
  • Selected candidates must undergo Pre-Employment Medical Examination

Note: Mere fulfilling minimum eligibility criteria does not entitle candidate for selection. RCF Ltd reserves the right to cancel/modify the advertisement without assigning any reason.

How to Apply for RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official RCFL website (www.rcfltd.com) → HR → RECRUITMENT → APPLY ONLINE or through IBPS portal. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online portal is active from 11-04-2026 to 13-05-2026.

Steps to Apply Online:

  1. Step 1: Visit Official Website - Go to www.rcfltd.com → HR → RECRUITMENT → APPLY ONLINE or directly visit IBPS Portal for RCFL
  2. Step 2: Register - Register with Name, Contact details & Email-id to generate Provisional Registration Number & Password
  3. Step 3: Login - Login using generated Registration Number and Password
  4. Step 4: Fill Application Form - Fill all details in the application form accurately:
    • Personal details
    • Educational qualifications
    • Category details
    • Contact details
  5. Step 5: Upload Documents - Upload scanned copies of:
    • Recent passport-size photograph (as per specifications)
    • Signature (as per specifications)
    • Left Thumb Impression (as per specifications)
    • Hand-written Declaration (as per format given in notification)
    • Required documents in PDF format
  6. Step 6: Pay Application Fee - Pay application fee online (wherever applicable) through available payment modes
  7. Step 7: Submit Application - Review all details carefully and submit the application
  8. Step 8: Take Printout - Take printout of application form and e-receipt for future reference

Important Notes:

  • Keep email ID & mobile number active till completion of recruitment process
  • All communications will be sent via email/SMS only
  • Category once submitted cannot be changed

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Recent passport-size coloured photograph (as per specifications in notification)
  • Signature (scanned as per specifications)
  • Left Thumb Impression (scanned as per specifications)
  • Hand-written Declaration in English (as per format in notification)

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Document Scrutiny (on being called):

  • Printout of online application form
  • Valid Call Letter
  • Original Photo Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Passport/Driving License/Voter ID etc.)
  • Educational qualification certificates:
    • 10th certificate and mark sheet (for date of birth proof)
    • 12th certificate and mark sheet (if applicable)
    • B.Sc. (Chemistry) degree and mark sheets with Physics as subject (Option 1)
    • Diploma certificate and mark sheets (Option 2 & 3)
    • NCVT certificate in AO(CP) Trade (Option 1)
    • BOAT training completion certificate (Option 2)
  • Category certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS) in prescribed proforma issued by competent authority
  • PwBD certificate (if applicable) issued by competent authority
  • Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate (if applicable)
  • Certificate for children/family members of victims of 1984 riots (if applicable)
  • NOC from current employer (for candidates employed in Government/PSU)
  • Any other relevant documents as mentioned in notification

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/rcflfeb26/

Important Instructions for RCFL Operator Trainee Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply
  • Age and qualification criteria calculated as on crucial date i.e. 01-02-2026
  • Candidates in final year of Graduation/Post Graduation are not eligible
  • Only full time and regular courses are acceptable. Distance/Correspondence/Part-time courses not eligible
  • Minimum percentage requirement: 55% for General/EWS/OBC (NCL), 50% for SC/ST in last year/aggregate of last two semesters
  • Category once submitted cannot be changed at any stage
  • Reserved category candidates must submit valid Caste/EWS/Disability certificate in prescribed proforma
  • Candidates employed in Government/PSUs must submit NOC at Skill Test stage
  • Application fee is non-refundable under any circumstances
  • Keep email ID and mobile number active till completion of recruitment process
  • All communications will be sent via email/SMS only - no hard copy will be sent by post
  • Document Scrutiny will be conducted in the ratio of 1:7 (Vacancy : Candidates)
  • Skill Test is qualifying in nature - minimum 60 marks required for General/EWS/OBC (NCL), 55 marks for SC/ST
  • Final selection is based on Computer Based Online Test marks only (100% weightage)
  • Selected candidates must undergo Pre-Employment Medical Examination and must be found medically fit
  • Mere fulfilling minimum eligibility criteria does not entitle candidate for selection
  • Selected candidates will undergo 6 months training and will receive consolidated stipend of Rs. 22,000/- per month during training
  • Hostel accommodation/quarter on sharing basis will be provided free of cost during training period
  • After successful completion of training, candidates will be absorbed in Grade II-(A6) with pay scale Rs. 22,000 - 60,000
  • The number of vacancies is subject to variation based on business requirements
  • RCF Ltd reserves the right to cancel/modify the advertisement without assigning any reason
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification
  • Any dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Mumbai courts only

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment: Get the latest Indian Air Force recruitment notification for commissioned officer vacancies in Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) Branches commencing July 2027. Online applications open on 20th May 2026 (1100 Hrs) and close on 19th June 2026 (2330 Hrs). Apply through the official website https://afcat.cdac.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 apply online from the official AFCAT career page.

FOLLOW US

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Indian Air Force has officially released the AFCAT – 02/2026/ NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Entry notification for grant of Short Service Commission (SSC) in Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) Branches for courses commencing in July 2027. Online applications are also invited for NCC Special Entry Scheme (for Flying Branch) and Graduate Aptitude Test for Engineering (GATE) Score Entry (for Ground Duty Technical branches). Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification and detailed information are available on the official AFCAT website.

  • Download IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Employment News Notification PDF

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment Overview

Organization Name Indian Air Force (IAF), Ministry of Defence, Govt. of India
Notification AFCAT – 02/2026 / NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Entry
Post Name Commissioned Officer (Flying Branch & Ground Duty – Technical and Non-Technical)
Type of Commission Permanent Commission (PC) and Short Service Commission (SSC) for Men & Women
Course Commencement July 2027
Application Mode Online only (through https://afcat.cdac.in)
AFCAT Exam Fee Rs. 550/- + GST (Non-Refundable) | NCC Special Entry & GATE Score Entry: NIL
Pay Scale (on Commissioning) Flying Officer: Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500/- (Level 10 as per 7th CPC) + MSP Rs. 15,500/-
Official Website https://afcat.cdac.in

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment Important Dates

Event Date
Opening of Online Registration 20-05-2026 (1100 Hrs)
Last Date for Online Registration 19-06-2026 (2330 Hrs)
Course Commencement July 2027

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Branch, Course Number and Vacancy Details

Vacancies indicated are tentative and subject to change without notice. Actual intake would depend on organizational requirements, various cadre control mechanisms, availability of training slots, and actual number of vacancies accruing in July 2027. No representation in this regard will be entertained. Two seats are reserved from Ground Duty (Non-Technical) vacancies for Law qualified candidates.

Branch Vacancies
Flying Officer 300+ (Expected)

 

Entry Branch Course Number
AFCAT Entry Flying 222/27F/SSC/M&W
Ground Duty (Technical) 221/27T/PC/, 111AEC/M&W, 221/27T/SSC/, 111AEC/M&W
Ground Duty (Non-Technical) 221/27G/SSC/M&W
NCC Special Entry Flying 222/27F/PC/M and 222/27F/SSC/M&W
GATE Score Entry Ground Duty (Technical) 221/27T/PC/, 111AEC/M&W, 221/27T/SSC/, 111AEC/M&W

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Type of Commission

Type Details
Permanent Commission (PC) – Men & Women Candidates joining as PC would continue to serve till the age of superannuation in their respective branches as per their rank.
SSC – Flying Branch (Men & Women) Engagement period for Flying Branch SSC officers is 14 years from the date of commissioning.
SSC – Ground Duty Technical & Non-Technical (Men & Women) Initial tenure for SSC officers in Ground Duty branches would be for a period of 10 years. An extension of 4 years may be granted subject to service requirements, availability of vacancies, willingness, suitability, and merit.
Grant of PC (at a later date) Considered subject to service requirements, availability of vacancies, willingness, suitability, merit, and as per prevailing policies on the subject.

Note: SSC officers are not entitled for grant of pension.

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Eligibility Criteria (Age)

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 01 July 2027. All age calculations are done with reference to 01 July 2027.

Branch / Entry Age Limit
Flying Branch (AFCAT Entry) 20 to 24 years as on 01 July 2027 (born between 02 July 2003 and 01 July 2007, both dates inclusive). Upper age limit for candidates holding valid and current Commercial Pilot Licence issued by DGCA (India) is relaxable up to 26 years (born between 02 July 2001 to 01 July 2007, both dates inclusive).
Ground Duty (Technical & Non-Technical) – AFCAT Entry and Ground Duty (Technical) – GATE Score Entry 20 to 26 years as on 01 July 2027 (born between 02 July 2001 and 01 July 2007, both dates inclusive).

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Educational Qualifications

Details on physical/medical standards, educational qualifications, and the full selection procedure are available in the detailed notification on the website. Candidates are advised to visit the official AFCAT website for branch-wise educational qualification requirements.

Branch Educational Qualification (General)
Flying Branch Graduation with Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level, or BE/B.Tech degree. (Refer official notification for full details.)
Ground Duty (Technical) BE/B.Tech in relevant engineering disciplines. GATE Score Entry candidates must have valid GATE scores in specified disciplines. (Refer official notification for full details.)
Ground Duty (Non-Technical) Graduation in relevant discipline as per the sub-branch (Administration, Logistics, Accounts, Education, Meteorology, Weather Service). (Refer official notification for full details.)

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Pay, Allowances & Privileges

IAF Offers the following pay and benefits on commissioning as per 7th CPC:

Rank Pay as per Defence Matrix Level MSP (Military Service Pay)
Flying Officer Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500/- 10 Rs. 15,500/-

Note: Flight Cadets shall receive a fixed stipend of Rs. 56,100/- per month during one year of training.

Allowances: On commissioning, allowances are applicable based on the branch/nature of posting including Flying Allowance, Transport Allowance, Technical Allowance, Test Pilot & Flight Test Engineer Allowance, Risk and Hardship Allowance, etc.

Privileges: Air Force Officers are entitled to furnished accommodation, comprehensive medical cover for self and dependents, Canteen, Officers' Mess, loans at subsidised rates, LTC, Leave (60 days annual and 20 days casual), subject to service exigencies.

Insurance: Mandatory Insurance Cover is applicable for IAF Officers through Air Force Group Insurance Society (AFGIS). Group Insurance for death while in service is Rs. 1.25 Crore (on contribution). Similarly, PC Officers retiring from service after rendering 20 or more years of service and SSC Officers who have completed 14 years of service are covered under Air Veterans Insurance Cover (AVIC) for Rs. 24 Lacs initially for 15 years, after retirement. The annual transfer for this is Rs. 12 Lacs till the age of 75 years.

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Examination Fee

Entry Type Examination Fee
AFCAT Entry (Men & Women) Rs. 550/- + GST (Non-Refundable)
NCC Special Entry NIL (No Examination Fee)
GATE Score Entry NIL (No Examination Fee)

IAF AFCAT 02/2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 is merit-based. Candidates registering for AFCAT entry will have to pay the examination fee and appear for the AFCAT Online Test conducted at various centres. Details of conduct of exam, examination centres, and mode of payment are available in the notification.

Stage Process
Stage 1 AFCAT Online Examination / NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Screening
Stage 2 Air Force Selection Board (AFSB) Interview (including Psychological Test, Group Tests, and Interview) at various AFSB centres
Stage 3 Medical Examination at Air Force Central Medical Establishment (AFCME) / Institute of Aviation Medicine (IAM)
Stage 4 Final Merit List and Joining Instructions

Physical Conditioning: Candidates are advised to be physically fit when reporting for SSB. Aim to achieve an ability to run 01 mile (1.6 km) in 10 minutes, do 10 push-ups and 03 chin-ups. Prospective candidates are also advised to keep themselves in good physical condition by training at RFA which encompasses running, swimming, rope climbing, and other forms of physical training/conditioning. Mandatory tests are conducted during training.

How to Apply for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment

Interested candidates should log on to https://afcat.cdac.in to apply. No other means/mode of application shall be accepted. Candidates are required to possess a valid email ID and active mobile phone number throughout the selection process. Online registration will open on 20-05-2026 (1100 Hrs) and close on 19-06-2026 (2330 Hrs).

Candidates eligible for one or more entries are required to register separately for each entry. After applying online, the candidate is required to download the application slip generated by the system with a unique application number. Information filled in by candidates in the online application form will be considered final and no amendments will be allowed subsequently. Candidates should exercise due care while entering their date of birth.

Important Points Before Registration:

  • Candidates must maintain their unique registered Email ID and mobile phone number active throughout the selection process.
  • PAN Card and account in SBI/Nationalised Bank is mandatory at the time of joining Air Force Academy.
  • Aadhaar Card is mandatory for registration.
  • Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of the course. Marriage during the period of training shall result in discharge and liability to refund all expenditure incurred by the Government.

Apply Online at: https://afcat.cdac.in

For Queries: Related to conduct of online examination, registration process, and admit cards — contact +91 9513252077 or E-Mail [email protected]. For queries regarding eligibility, allotment of AFSB centres, conduct of AFSB interview, merit list, and Joining Instructions — contact 011-23010231 Extn 7610 or Toll free number 1800-11-2448.

Important Instructions for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Citizens as per Citizenship Act 1955 are eligible to apply.
  • Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of the course.
  • Permanent body tattoos are not permitted on any other body part except on the inner face of forearms (elbow to wrist) and on the reverse side (palm/back dorsal) of both hands. Self-declaration certificate is mandatory for small innocuous tattoos that are not prejudicial to good order and military discipline.
  • Use/possession of narcotics is banned. Candidates found using narcotics at any time during training will be debarred from joining the IAF.
  • Information filled in online application is final — no amendments allowed subsequently. Exercise due care while entering date of birth.
  • Travel Allowance (TA) to and fro for travelling to AFSB will be reimbursed at AC-III Tier/AC Chair Car or actual normal bus fare for first-time candidates. No TA is admissible if you have already appeared for the same type of entry at any of the Selection Boards.
  • Candidates must furnish correct and active Saving Bank Account details (not SB Account like Sukanya Samridhi etc.) for transfer of TA claims.
  • Candidates are advised to keep themselves regularly updated through the official AFCAT website regarding all selection process alerts.
  • Selection in the Indian Air Force is fair and merit-based. Any attempt to influence the selection process at any stage or use of any unfair means like forgery, impersonation, or supplying false information is liable to lead to termination of candidature or initiation of service and legal action against the candidate.
  • Terms and conditions given in the advertisement are only guidelines and are subject to change without notice.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB) has officially announced the Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 with 91 openings. The detailed notification was published on 09 May 2026 at www.hpscb.bank.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18–45 years (with relaxations for reserved categories) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline along with the required cooperative society experience can now submit their online applications from 09 May 2026 to 29 May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below

FOLLOW US

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Head Office - The Mall, Shimla (H.P.) - 171001 has officially released the detailed notification for recruitment to 91 posts of Junior Clerks (quota for PACS/OCS of HP) on Regular Basis through IBPS, Mumbai. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination pattern, syllabus, PwBD guidelines, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official HPSCB website.

  • Download HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Shimla - 171001
Post Name Junior Clerk (quota for PACS/OCS of HP)
Conducting Body IBPS, Mumbai (Online Examination conducted through IBPS)
Quota Type PACS Quota (Trained Secretaries of Primary Agricultural Cooperative Societies of HP) and OCS Quota (Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries)
Total Vacancies 91 (PACS Quota: 74 + OCS Quota: 17)
Pay Scale Minimum Pay of Rs. 19,900/- (Level-2) + admissible allowances per month (Regular Basis)
Application Mode Online only (via HPSCB website - www.hpscb.bank.in)
Selection Mode Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes) + Document Verification/Scrutiny (No Personal Interview)
Mandatory Requirement Bonafide Himachali for all reserved categories; valid experience certificate from cooperative society (PACS: min. 3 years; OCS: min. 5 years) countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P.
Official Website https://www.hpscb.bank.in

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Opening Date for Submission of Online Application 09-05-2026
Last Date for Submission of Online Application 29-05-2026
Application Fee Payment Window 09-05-2026 to 29-05-2026 (Online Mode Only)
Date of Downloading of Call Letters Around 7 days before examination date
Date of Online Examination Expected 5-6 weeks after last date of submission (exact date to be notified on Bank's website)
Date of Declaration of Result for Online Examination Around 15 days after conduct of examination
Age Calculated As On 01-01-2026

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 91 vacancies have been announced for the post of Junior Clerk under two quota categories - PACS Quota (74 posts) and OCS Quota (17 posts). These are distributed across General (UR), EWS, SC, ST, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, IRDP, WFF, and PwBD categories. All reserved category benefits apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. Number of posts may vary depending on actual requirement at the time of final selection.

PACS Quota - Category-wise Vacancy (Quota reserved for Trained Secretaries of PACS of HP)

Category No. of Vacancies
GEN / UR 19
EWS of HP 07
SC of HP 12
ST of HP 02
OBC of HP 11

SC (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 03

OBC-IRDP of HP 02

Sub-Total (PACS Quota) 74

OCS Quota - Category-wise Vacancy (Quota reserved for Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries)

Category No. of Vacancies
GEN / UR 06
EWS of HP 02

Grand Total: 91 Posts (PACS: 74 + OCS: 17)

Sl. No. Sub-Category of PwBD Post No. of Posts
1 Blindness and Low Vision 01
2 Deaf and Hard of Hearing 01
3 Locomotor Disability including Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims and Muscular Dystrophy 01

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 01-01-2026. All reserved category candidates must be Bonafide Himachalis. Reservation benefits are applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate from competent authority. Candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBDs/Ex-SM and other reserved categories of HP may apply against unreserved/general category posts but will not be eligible for age relaxation or fee concession in such cases. Candidates must also ensure they are not directly or indirectly a defaulter of their society.

Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. with authentic certification. Such certificate must be supported by: (i) Copy of resolution of the managing committee for initial appointment, and (ii) Copy of relevant pages of statutory audit report/note for prescribed years showing payment of wages/salary from the society account.

Educational Qualification

Post / Quota Essential Qualifications
Junior Clerk (PACS Quota - Trained Secretaries of PACS of HP) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Candidates must have passed Matriculation and 10+2 from a School/Institution within Himachal Pradesh - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 3 years continuous service as Secretary as defined in Rule 2(vii) of H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules, 1971 of Primary Agriculture Credit Society; (iii) All employees of PACS covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society
Junior Clerk (OCS Quota - Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Matriculation and 10+2 from HP school - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 5 years continuous service in a cooperative society; (iii) All employees of other cooperative societies and PACS employees not covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society

Types of PACS and OCS covered under this recruitment:

Quota Type Societies Covered
PACS (Primary Agricultural Credit Societies) Cooperative Multipurpose Societies Ltd.; Gram Service Cooperative Societies Ltd.
OCS (Other Cooperative Societies) Fruit Growers and Marketing Society Ltd.; Marketing and Consumer Cooperative Societies Ltd.; Cooperative Consumer Store; Thrift & Credit Society Ltd.; All other Cooperative Societies registered under HP Cooperative Societies Act, 1968

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be above 18 years and below 45 years of age as on 01-01-2026. Candidates born between 02-01-1981 and 01-01-2008 (both days inclusive) are eligible. Age relaxation is applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates of the respective reserved categories. Selected PwBD candidates will have to submit a Medical Certificate from a competent authority confirming physical fitness to perform duties.

Category Age Relaxation
General (UR) 18 to 45 years (as on 01-01-2026)
SC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
ST of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
OBC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
EWS of HP As per HP Govt. norms
Physically Handicapped (PwBD) 5 years relaxation (minimum 40% disability required)
Ward of Freedom Fighter (WFF) of HP 5 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen (Reserved Vacancies) Age at time of joining military service should not exceed upper age limit; present age must be less than 58 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)
Ex-Servicemen (Unreserved Vacancies) Actual age minus approved military service must not exceed prescribed maximum age by more than 3 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Junior Clerk are required to pay a non-refundable application fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the HPSCB application portal. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Category Application Fee
General (UR) and OBC of H.P. Rs. 1,000/- (Rupees One Thousand only) - Non-refundable
EWS of H.P. (Economically Weaker Sections) Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
SC / ST / IRDP / BPL / ANTODAYA of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
Female Candidates of All Categories of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 consists of 2 stages - Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes, conducted through IBPS Mumbai) followed by Document Verification/Scrutiny. There is no personal interview. Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification. Final selection will be made strictly on the basis of merit in the online examination after document verification. Candidates must score minimum 40% marks in the online examination to be shortlisted for document verification. Candidates declared successful who do not personally attend document verification will have their candidature cancelled.

Stage 1 - Online Examination (150 Marks, 150 Minutes)

Sl. No. Subject No. of Questions Marks Duration
1 Reasoning 30 30 150 Minutes (2½ Hours) - No separate time limit per section
2 Numerical Ability 30 30
3 English 30 30
4 General Awareness (including HP General Knowledge) 30 30
5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules 30 30
Total 150 150  

Stage 2 - Document Verification / Scrutiny

Shortlisting Ratio Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification
Minimum Qualifying Marks 40% of total marks (i.e., minimum 60 out of 150) in online examination
No Negative Marking There is NO negative marking in the online examination
Question Paper Type Objective type multiple choice (MCQ) - bilingual (English and Hindi)

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Preference to candidates possessing higher academic qualifications
2nd Tie-Break Preference to candidates belonging to the State of H.P.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

The Online Examination consists of 5 sections - Reasoning, Numerical Ability, English, General Awareness, and H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and Rules - each carrying 30 marks (total 150 marks, 150 minutes). The level of examination and syllabus is in accordance with the minimum educational qualification criteria i.e. Graduation level. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi) and is of objective type MCQ. There is no negative marking. Preference will be given to candidates possessing knowledge of customs, traditions, languages, and culture of Himachal Pradesh and the test shall include questions on such subjects.

Section Topic Key Coverage Marks
Section 1 Reasoning Logical Reasoning, Analytical Reasoning, Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning 30
Section 2 Numerical Ability Quantitative Aptitude, Number Systems, Arithmetic, Data Interpretation 30
Section 3 English Reading Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary, Sentence Completion 30
Section 4 General Awareness (including HP GK) General Awareness, Current Affairs, HP General Knowledge, Customs, Traditions, Languages and Culture of Himachal Pradesh 30
Section 5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act & H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules Provisions of HP Cooperative Societies Act and HP Cooperative Societies Rules 30

How to Apply for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.hpscb.bank.in by clicking the "APPLY ONLINE" option. Hard copy applications or applications by any other mode will not be accepted. The online application window is open from 09-05-2026 to 29-05-2026. Candidates must register on the portal, make fee payment online, and upload all required documents. No change will be possible after clicking the "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" button. Candidates are advised to keep their valid personal email ID and mobile number active till the completion of the entire recruitment process.

Before applying, candidates must have ready: scanned photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm), signature (with black ink on white paper), left thumb impression (on white paper with black or blue ink), and a hand-written declaration in English on white paper with black ink. Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS will NOT be accepted. The hand-written declaration text is: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

Documents Required to be Produced at Document Verification (when called):

  • Graduation degree certificate and mark sheets from recognized University
  • Matriculation and 10+2 certificates from HP school/institution (if applicable)
  • Experience Certificate countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. - with copy of managing committee resolution for initial appointment and statutory audit report pages (3 years for PACS quota; 5 years for OCS quota)
  • Age proof (10th/12th class mark sheet/certificate)
  • Bonafide Himachali Certificate from competent authority (for reserved category candidates)
  • SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/WFF/IRDP certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) Certificate (for OBC candidates) - in latest prescribed format
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate issued by Deputy Commissioner/SDO (Civil)/Tehsildar (for EWS candidates) - family income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum
  • PwBD Certificate (minimum 40% disability) + Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty (for PwBD candidates)
  • Discharge Certificate (for Ex-Servicemen candidates)
  • WFF Certificate (for Ward of Freedom Fighter candidates)
  • IRDP/BPL/Antodaya Certificate countersigned by Block Development Officer (if applicable)
  • NOC and Good Character Certificate from current employer (for candidates in regular/confirmed service)
  • e-Receipt and printout of online application form

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/hpscbmar26/

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Link and Form Details

The online application for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 is to be filled and submitted through the Bank's official website. The step-by-step process is as follows:

Step Action Details
Step 1 Visit Bank's Website Go to www.hpscb.bank.in → Click "APPLY ONLINE" → Choose "Click here for New Registration" → Enter Name, Contact Details, and Email ID → Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password generated
Step 2 Fill the Application Form Fill all personal details, category, qualification, and experience details. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save data if unable to complete in one go. Details must match exactly as in certificates/mark sheets.
Step 3 Upload Documents Upload: (1) Photograph (4.5×3.5 cm, 20-50 KB, JPG); (2) Signature (black ink, 10-20 KB, JPG); (3) Left Thumb Impression (black/blue ink on white paper, 20-50 KB, JPG); (4) Hand-written Declaration (black ink on white paper, English only, 50-100 KB, JPG). Capture live webcam photo as required.
Step 4 Preview and Complete Registration Click "Preview Tab" to verify all details → Modify if required → Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" only after verifying everything is correct. No changes allowed after this step.
Step 5 Pay Application Fee Click "Payment" Tab → Pay online via Debit Card (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, or Mobile Wallets → Wait for server intimation (do not press Back/Refresh) → Download e-Receipt
Step 6 Submit and Print Click "Submit" → Take printout of e-Receipt and online application form with fee details → Download Call Letter (around 7 days before exam) from www.hpscb.bank.in using Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth

For queries: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/hpscbmar26/

Important Instructions for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • All reserved category benefits including age relaxation and fee concession apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates.
  • Candidates must possess valid experience certificate from their cooperative society - false experience certificates will result in summary cancellation of candidature irrespective of qualifying the online examination.
  • Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. supported by managing committee resolution and statutory audit report pages.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only via www.hpscb.bank.in.
  • Application fee is non-refundable; fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS and hand-written declaration not in candidate's own handwriting or not in English will be treated as invalid.
  • Candidates already in regular (confirmed) service must submit NOC and good character certificate from current employer at document verification - absence of NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • There is NO negative marking in the online examination; minimum 40% marks required to qualify.
  • Candidates must appear in person at document verification - non-appearance will lead to cancellation of candidature without notice.
  • OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate; EWS candidates must have family gross annual income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum.
  • PwBD candidates must have minimum 40% degree of disability and must submit Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty at selected post.
  • Wards of Ex-Servicemen are NOT eligible for posts advertised for Ex-Servicemen.
  • Selected candidates will be on probation for a period of two years (extendable up to maximum 3 years).
  • No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in the online examination.
  • Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in the area of operation of H.P. State Cooperative Bank in Himachal Pradesh.
  • For any dispute related to selection process, the decision of the Registrar, Co-operative Societies of Himachal Pradesh shall be binding and final; jurisdiction shall be Shimla (HP) only.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.hpscb.bank.in regularly for all updates regarding the recruitment process.

Rural / Semi-Urban Rs. 15,000/-
Urban Rs. 18,000/-
Metro Rs. 20,000/-

How to Apply for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the BFSI SSC portal. Hard copy of application and other documents must not be sent to the Bank's office. Candidates are requested to apply online through https://unionbankofindia.bank.in/recruitment.aspx or https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_register/mkt. All candidates must also register on the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/student_type.php) before applying and note their Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS) and Enrolment ID (NATS) for future correspondence.

Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained, and fee paid against other applications will stand forfeited. No change of data is allowed after final submission of the online application.

Documents to be Kept Ready Before Applying Online:

  • Aadhaar card front and back side (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB each)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and active Mobile Number
  • Passport size photograph (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • Qualifying Marksheet / Degree / Provisional Certificate (Format: PDF, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • Caste Certificate, if applicable (Format: PDF, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • PwBD Certificate, if applicable (Format: PDF, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • PAN Card (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • APAAR ID Card (Format: JPEG, Size: Less than 1 MB)
  • NAPS Apprentice Registration Code and NATS Enrolment Number

Examination fee can be paid by Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / UPI. On successful payment, an e-receipt will be sent to the registered email ID. Call letters for online examination will be communicated via email from [email protected]. No hard copy of the call letter will be sent by post. For queries: [email protected] or [email protected].

Apply Online at: https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_register/mkt

Important Instructions for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Engagement as Apprentice is purely for training and does not guarantee any employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already undergone or are undergoing Apprenticeship training are not eligible.
  • Candidates with more than one year job experience after graduation are not eligible.
  • Only one application should be submitted. Multiple applications will lead to rejection.
  • Proficiency in the local language of the applied state is mandatory.
  • Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify the candidature.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The South Indian Bank Limited, a Premier Scheduled Commercial Bank in India, has officially released a recruitment notification for the post of Junior Officer (Operations) for the Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions covering the states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The notification includes complete details on eligibility, terms of employment, salary/compensation, mode of selection, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website.

  • Download South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name The South Indian Bank Ltd.
Post Name Junior Officer (Operations)
Recruitment Region Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions (States of Andhra Pradesh & Telangana)
Type of Employment Contract - initially for 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performing candidates may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period
Pay / CTC Rs. 4.86 lakhs to Rs. 5.04 lakhs per annum (inclusive of NPS contribution, Insurance Premium and Variable Pay)
Application Mode Online only (via South Indian Bank official recruitment portal)
Selection Mode Online Test and/or Group Discussion + Preliminary and/or Final Interview
Application Fee NIL
Official Website https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Online Application - Start Date 06-05-2026
Online Application - End Date 13-05-2026
Eligibility Criteria Calculated As On 31-03-2026
Online Test / Group Discussion Date To be notified on Bank's official website
Interview Date To be notified on Bank's official website

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 31.03.2026. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for the Junior Officers role as per any previous notification of the Bank and were not included in the final selection list are not eligible to apply for this notification. Proficiency in local language (Telugu) will be given preference. All eligibility is provisional and subject to verification of original documents when a candidate reports for interview/training/joining.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Minimum Qualification Any Graduation with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university.
Stream of Education Regular stream only: SSLC/SSC, HSC and Graduation (10+2+ Graduation under regular stream).
Work Experience Minimum 1 year of experience in Bank / NBFCs.
Special Note Candidates who have attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per previous notification(s) and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

The maximum age limit is 28 years as on 31.03.2026. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST candidates. Candidates belonging to SC/ST must submit attested copies of certificates from a competent authority to prove their status as and when called for.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) Maximum 28 years (as on 31.03.2026)
SC / ST 5 years of age relaxation (maximum 33 years)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

No application fee is required for this post. Candidates can apply free of cost through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal.

Category Recruitment Fee
All Categories NIL (No Application Fee)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Terms of Employment

Selected candidates will be appointed on a contract basis initially for a period of 3 years, renewable at the sole discretion of the Bank based on performance evaluation. On completion of the initial contract period or any extension, high performing candidates may, without any obligation and in the Bank's sole and absolute discretion, be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I), subject to the then prevailing norms of the Bank. There will be no mandatory minimum service period as stipulated in the case of staff members recruited under regular employment.

Parameter Details
Designation / Job Role Junior Officer (Operations)
Term of Appointment Contract - initially 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performers may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period, at Bank's sole discretion
Posting Location Anywhere in Andhra Pradesh & Telangana as per Bank's requirement; liable to be transferred anywhere in India at Bank's sole discretion
Total CTC Rs. 4.86 lakhs to Rs. 5.04 lakhs per annum (inclusive of NPS contribution, Insurance Premium and Variable Pay based on performance)
Official Duty Allowance Eligible Lodging, Halting and Travelling allowance for official travel, as per Bank norms
Insurance Group Medical Insurance and Group Accident Insurance at the cost of the Bank; Group Life Insurance with premium borne by the employee
Increment Annual increment during contract period based on performance evaluation or other parameters as decided by the Bank

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 consists of an Online Test and/or Group Discussion, followed by a Preliminary and/or Final Interview. The Online Test and Group Discussion are elimination stages - only test-qualified candidates will be called for Group Discussion or Personal Interview, and only candidates shortlisted from the Group Discussion will be advanced to the Personal Interview. The Bank reserves the right to modify the selection process based on the number of applications received. Mere eligibility will not vest any right on the applicant to be called for the Recruitment Process. All marks/scores/grades at any stage shall be treated as strictly confidential.

Stage Details Nature
Stage 1 Online Test (if conducted) Elimination Stage - conducted at locations identified by the Bank
Stage 2 Group Discussion (if conducted) Elimination Stage - only Online Test qualified candidates called
Stage 3 Preliminary and/or Final Personal Interview Only Group Discussion shortlisted candidates advanced

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) posts can do so by submitting their application form online. The application process is online only through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the closing date.

The application form for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/. Eligible candidates with any Graduation (minimum 50% marks) and minimum 1 year of experience in Bank/NBFCs are encouraged to fill out the online application form and upload the required documents before the last date.

Online Apply Link for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

How to Apply for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

Applicants can apply online only through the Bank's official recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/ from 06-05-2026 to 13-05-2026. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before registering, applicants must ensure they have a valid Email ID and mobile number in their name. An applicant can only apply for vacancies in one State - making multiple registrations will result in disqualification.

There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - candidates are requested to fill in the application form with utmost care. After successful submission, a system generated User ID (Application Ref. ID) will be generated - candidates must note this for future reference. An email with the application PDF and login credentials will be sent to the registered email ID upon successful submission. The physical copy of the application need not be sent to the Bank.

Documents / Upload Specifications:

  • Photograph: Recent passport size colour photo, white background, no caps/hats/dark glasses - Resolution: 378 x 437 pixels, maximum 50 KB (JPEG/JPG format)
  • Signature: Signed on white paper with black ink pen - Resolution: 140 x 110 pixels, maximum 50 KB
  • Resume: PDF format, file size not exceeding 1 MB
  • Education Documents: Certificates and marklists of X/SSLC (must include D.O.B), XII/HSC and final marklist and certificate of Graduation - combined as a single PDF file, not exceeding 3 MB

Documents to be Produced When Called for (Originals):

  • Proof of age (SSLC/SSC certificate with Date of Birth)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marklists (X, XII, Graduation)
  • Work experience certificate(s) proving minimum 1 year in Bank/NBFCs
  • SC/ST certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Proof of nationality / domicile as applicable
  • Any other documents as required by the Bank

For queries, contact: Toll Free: 1800-425-1809 / 1800-102-9408, 0484-277-1383 | Email: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/

Important Instructions for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Beware of fraudulent job alerts - apply only through the official online portal. Applications through any other mode will not be accepted.
  • Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per any previous notification and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.
  • An applicant can apply for vacancies in only one State - multiple registrations will lead to disqualification.
  • There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - fill in all details carefully before submission.
  • No application fee is required for this post.
  • Appointment is subject to Medical fitness, satisfactory background verification including CIBIL/credit history check, police clearance, and completion of other formalities as per Bank rules.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Applicants must appear for the Recruitment Process at their own cost.
  • Call letters/advice will be sent by email only - no hard copy will be sent. Candidates are advised to check their registered email and the Bank's official website regularly.
  • All marks/scores/grades obtained at any stage of the selection process are strictly confidential and will not be disclosed to any party including candidates.
  • Applicants are advised to avoid last-minute rush and submit the online application well before the closing date, as website access may be delayed due to heavy internet traffic near the deadline.
  • The Bank reserves the right to make modifications to the selection process and the qualifying criteria at any stage, and such decisions will be final and binding.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026: Get the latest RBI recruitment notification 2026 for 60 Officer in Grade 'B' (DR) vacancies under the Reserve Bank of India Services Board (General/DEPR/DSIM Cadres) - Panel Year 2026. The online application process starts on 29th April 2026 and the last date to apply online is 20th May 2026. Find RBI vacancies across India on the official website rbi.org.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 apply online from the RBI career page.

FOLLOW US

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Reserve Bank of India Services Board has officially released Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27 for recruitment to 60 posts of Officers in Grade 'B' (Direct Recruit-DR) (On Probation-OP) under the General, DEPR, and DSIM Cadres – Panel Year 2026. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination syllabus, PwBD category classification, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification and its annexures before applying online. The notification PDF and syllabus are available on the official RBI website.

  • Download RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Overview

Field Details
Commission/Organization Name Reserve Bank of India Services Board (RBISB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27
Post Name Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) (On Probation) – General / DEPR / DSIM Cadres
Department Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
Service General Cadre / DEPR Cadre / DSIM Cadre
Total Vacancies 60 (including PwBD posts)
Pay Scale Starting Basic Pay ₹78,450/- p.m. in the pay scale of ₹78450-4050(9)-114900-EB-4050(2)-123000-4650(4)-141600 (16 years); Gross emoluments approx. ₹1,54,936/- p.m. (without HRA)
Application Mode Online only (via RBI website www.rbi.org.in)
Selection Mode Phase-I Online Examination + Phase-II Online/Written Examination + Interview
Mandatory Requirement Eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026; valid nationality certificate where applicable
Official Website https://www.rbi.org.in

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 29/04/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 29-04-2026
Last Date for Online Application Submission 20-05-2026 (till 06:00 PM)
Correction Window (Edit Window) 2 days from the closing date of application submission
Age Calculated As On 01-04-2026
Phase-I Exam – General Cadre 13-06-2026
Phase-I Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 14-06-2026
Phase-II Exam – General Cadre 25-07-2026
Phase-II Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 26-07-2026

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 60 vacancies have been announced across three cadres – General, DEPR, and DSIM. Vacancies are distributed across GEN/UR, EWS, OBC, SC, and ST categories.

Post Total
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Cadre 40
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Cadre 10
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Cadre 10
Total 60

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) Sub-Category Vacancy

Sr. No. Post PwBD Category A PwBD Category B PwBD Category C PwBD Category D
1 Grade 'B' (DR) – General – – 1 1
2 Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR – – – –
3 Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM 1 – – –

Category A: Blindness & Low Vision; B: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; C: Locomotor disability; D: Autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability and mental illness

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026. Nationality conditions apply – the post is open to citizens of India and certain other categories as specified in the notification, subject to eligibility certificate from the Government of India where required.

The educational qualification must be from a University incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature, or an institution declared Deemed University under UGC Act, 1956, or an equivalent foreign university recognized by the Association of Indian Universities.

Educational Qualification

Post Minimum Educational Qualifications (as on April 01, 2026)
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Graduation in any discipline with minimum 60% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) OR Post-Graduation with minimum 55% marks (pass marks for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) in aggregate of all semesters/years.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Essential: MA/MSc in Economics or related Economics/Finance disciplines with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved) from a recognized Indian or Foreign University. Desirable: Doctorate in Economics, or research/teaching experience, or publication in standard journals.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Essential: Master's Degree with minimum 55% marks in Statistics/Mathematics/Data Science/AI/ML or related fields OR Four-year Bachelor's Degree with minimum 60% marks in the above fields (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved). Desirable: Doctorate in related subjects; research/teaching experience and publications.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must have attained the age of 21 years and must not have attained the age of 30 years as on April 01, 2026 (born not earlier than April 02, 1996 and not later than April 01, 2005). The upper age limit is relaxed for various categories as detailed below.

General/EWS candidates who have already appeared six times for Phase-I Examination for this post in the past are not eligible to apply. No such restriction applies to SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates if posts are reserved for them.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) / EWS 21 to 30 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
OBC 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
PwBD – Gen/EWS 10 years relaxation (max 40 years)
PwBD – OBC 13 years relaxation (max 43 years)
PwBD – SC/ST 15 years relaxation (max 45 years)
Ex-Servicemen / Ex-Bank Employees 5 years relaxation
Candidates with M.Phil. Upper age limit 32 years
Candidates with Ph.D. Upper age limit 34 years
Bank Officers (experience-based relaxation) Up to 3 years relaxation; upper age not to exceed 33 years including experience
DEPR/DSIM – Master's with Research/Teaching Experience Up to 3 years relaxation

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Officers in Grade 'B' are required to pay a non-refundable fee/intimation charges as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the RBI application portal. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances nor held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

Candidates submitting applications without the prescribed fee/intimation charges will be summarily rejected. Candidates are therefore advised to confirm their eligibility and verify all details before making payment.

Category Charges Amount
SC / ST / PwBD Intimation Charges only ₹100/- + 18% GST
GEN / OBC / EWS Application Fee including Intimation Charges ₹850/- + 18% GST
RBI Staff (eligible Staff Candidates) Nil Nil

Bank/Transaction charges are to be borne by the candidate.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 consists of Phase-I Online Examination, Phase-II Online/Written Examination, and Interview. Candidates who qualify Phase-I will be shortlisted for Phase-II based on aggregate marks. Only those who clear Phase-II will be called for the Interview.

No candidate will be called for the Interview unless he/she appears in Phase-II examination. The final merit list will be prepared by adding marks secured in Phase-II and Interview (and grace marks as applicable). If a candidate remains absent in the Interview, his/her candidature will not be considered for final selection.

The selection list recommended by the Board remains valid for the period as decided by the Board/Bank.

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Single Paper General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning Objective MCQ 200 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online/Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Statistics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes

Stage 2 – Phase-II Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economic and Social Issues 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English (Writing Skills) Descriptive (typed) 100 90 Minutes
Paper-III General Finance and Management 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Grand Total     300  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Total     400 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-II Statistics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 180 Minutes
Paper-III English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 90 Minutes
Total     300 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 3 – Interview: 75 Marks (All Cadres)

Candidates will also undergo a Personality Assessment before the Interview. No marks are allotted for Personality Assessment and it does not form part of the final selection criteria. Candidates may opt for Interview in Hindi or English.

Qualifying Marks

Category Minimum Qualifying Marks
All Categories Minimum marks separately for each test/paper as prescribed by the Board; minimum aggregate cut-off for Phase-II shortlisting decided by the Board in relation to number of vacancies

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

Phase-I – General Cadre (200 Marks, Objective MCQ, 120 Minutes)

The paper covers General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning. Separate time will be allotted for each section. Detailed information will be provided in the Information Handout available with the Admit Card.

Phase-II – General Cadre

Paper-I (Economic and Social Issues): Growth and Development (National Income, poverty alleviation, sustainable development); Indian Economy (economic history, monetary and fiscal policy since 1991, Indian money and financial markets, public finance, agriculture, services sector); Globalization (BoP, export-import policy, IMF, World Bank, WTO); Social Structure in India (multiculturalism, demographic trends, urbanization, gender issues, social justice).

Paper-II (English – Writing Skills): Writing skills including expression and understanding of the topic.

Paper-III (General Finance and Management): Financial system (structure of financial institutions, RBI functions, banking system, digital payments); Financial markets (primary and secondary markets); General topics (financial risk management, derivatives, financial inclusion, corporate governance, Union Budget, accounting basics, inflation); Management (fundamentals, organizational behaviour, motivation, leadership, ethics, corporate governance, communication).

Phase-I – DEPR Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Economics)

Microeconomics, Macroeconomics (IS-LM Model, schools of economic thought), International Economics, Theories of Economic Growth and Development, Public Finance, Environmental Economics, Quantitative Methods in Economics, Current developments in Indian Economy.

Phase-II – DEPR Cadre (Descriptive – Economics)

Paper-I covers Microeconomics Module (consumer theory, production theory, market theory, welfare economics) and Macroeconomics Module (national income accounting, employment and output, inflation, money and banking, growth theories, international trade, public finance) with equal weightage. Paper-II covers Quantitative Methods in Economics (mathematical methods, statistical methods, econometrics) and Indian Economy – Policy and Trends (fiscal policy, monetary policy, banking sector, inflation, external sector, sectoral developments) with equal weightage.

Phase-I – DSIM Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Statistics) and Phase-II (Paper-II, Descriptive – Statistics)

Topics covered across both papers: Theory of Probability, Probability Distributions and Sampling Theory; Linear Models and Economic Statistics; Statistical Inference (Estimation, Testing of Hypothesis, Non-Parametric Tests); Stochastic Processes; Multivariate Analysis; Econometrics and Time Series; Optimization and Statistical Computing; Data Science, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Techniques; Database and Data Warehouse Management.

How to Apply for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.rbi.org.in. No other means/mode of application will be accepted. The online portal will be active from 29-04-2026 to 20-05-2026 (6:00 PM). Candidates desirous of applying for both Grade 'B' (DR)-General and Grade 'B' (DR)-DEPR or DSIM must apply using separate links and pay fees for both. Digi Locker has been integrated with the online application form for voluntary submission of credentials.

Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the application with the higher Registration ID (RID) will be considered valid, and fee paid against other RIDs will not be adjusted. After the closing date, a 2-day edit window will be available for making corrections in limited fields. Candidates are not required to upload supporting documents at the time of online application; documents will be verified at the Interview stage only.

Documents Required at Interview Stage:

  • Age proof (birth certificate or Class X marksheet/certificate)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets
  • SC / ST / OBC / PwBD / EWS / Ex-SM certificate (if applicable) in prescribed format
  • Photo identity proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Aadhaar, etc.)
  • Online application form printout with fee payment receipt

Candidates working in Government/PSU/Public Enterprises must inform their employer in writing before applying and submit an undertaking to this effect at the time of online application. At the time of joining, proper discharge certificates from their employer must be produced.

Apply Online at: https://www.rbi.org.in

Important Instructions for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only eligible citizens (as defined in the notification) are eligible to apply; nationality certificate required for certain categories.
  • Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted – online submission only.
  • Recruitment fee/intimation charges are non-refundable; applications without prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.
  • Government/PSU employees must submit an undertaking about informing their employer and bring discharge certificate at the time of joining.
  • Mobile phones, pagers, and all electronic devices are banned inside examination/interview premises; physical calculators are not permitted in any phase.
  • Candidates reporting after Gate Closure Time specified on the Admit Card will not be permitted to take the examination.
  • Candidates are advised to visit https://www.rbi.org.in regularly for updates; all corrigenda will be published on the Bank's website only.
  • Phase-I paper (General Cadre) will be set in English; all other papers (except English papers) will be set bilingually in Hindi and English.
  • Negative marking on MCQ questions will apply as prescribed; detailed marking scheme will be in the Information Handout.
  • For any problem in filling the form or downloading Admit Card, queries may be raised through http://cgrs.ibps.in mentioning the post and cadre in the subject line.
  • All decisions of the Board regarding eligibility, conduct of examinations, selection, and other matters are final and binding; any resultant dispute is subject to sole jurisdiction of Courts at Mumbai.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Application Fee 2026

  • Application fee is to be paid only through online mode via debit card, credit card, internet banking, UPI, IMPS, cash card or mobile wallet during 25 April 2026 to 15 May 2026.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Selection Process 2026

  • The selection process consists of an online Preliminary Examination (if required) and a Main Examination conducted by UPCISB.
  • Based on the Preliminary Exam, up to 15 times the number of vacancies will be shortlisted for the Main Exam.
  • On the basis of Main Exam result, up to 3 times the number of posts will be called for interview (where applicable) and up to 5 times the number of posts for proficiency test, wherever prescribed.
  • Both Preliminary and Main examinations will be objective-type online tests with multiple choice questions and negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer.

How to Apply for UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Recruitment 2026

  • Candidates must apply online only through the official website of U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board: https://www.upcisb.upsdc.gov.in.
  • First, complete “New Registration” by entering name, contact details and email ID to generate a registration number and password.
  • Fill in the online application form carefully using the “Save and Next” option, upload scanned photograph, signature, left-hand thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per the prescribed specifications.
  • Pay the application fee through the integrated payment gateway, ensure e-Receipt generation, and take a printout of the final submitted application form and fee receipt for future reference.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Salary 2026

  • The notification provides detailed pay scales for each post, such as specific minimum and maximum pay in the respective pay bands for Manager, Junior Manager, Assistant/Cashier, Assistant Manager, Accountant, Junior Engineer, Assistant Engineer and other posts.

  • Pay scales differ institution-wise and post-wise (for example, different pay bands for Junior Manager in different District Co-operative Banks), and all posts are permanent with regular pay as per the respective co-operative institution’s rules.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Candidates must thoroughly read and understand all instructions in the advertisement before filling the online application form.
  • The photograph, signature, thumb impression and handwritten declaration must be clear and as per the prescribed size and format, otherwise the application may be rejected.
  • No change is permitted in key details after clicking on “Complete Registration”, so all information (name, father’s name, etc.) must match official certificates.
  • Candidates should keep their registered email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process for receiving admit card and other communication from UPCISB.
  • Minimum efficiency standard (qualifying marks) and negative marking rules in the exam must be noted carefully, as incorrect answers will attract penalty.

How to Calculate Your Score Using SBI Clerk Mains Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for SBI Clerk Mains Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +1 mark (most sections), +2 marks for Computer Aptitude
  • Wrong Answer: -1/4 mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks

South East Central Railway (SECR) Recruitment 2026-27 has been announced for Trade Apprentice (Act Apprenticeship) posts! Published on 05 May 2026 at the official website secr.indianrailways.gov.in, this new recruitment drive offers 1644 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 15 to 24 years age group and possessing a 10th Pass with minimum 50% marks along with ITI in relevant trade can apply online from 05 May 2026 to 04 June 2026. Find all details about the SECR Trade Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Notification PDF

The Personnel Department, Divisional Railway Manager (DRM) Office, Raipur, South East Central Railway (SECR) has officially released the Act Apprentice Recruitment Notification (No: E/PB/R/Rectt/Act Appr./01/2026-27, dated 05.05.2026) for engagement of Trade Apprentices under the Apprentices Act-1961 and Apprenticeship Rules-1962 for the year 2026-27. Online applications are invited for engagement as Trade Apprentices in two establishments - DRM Office, Raipur Division (Estt. No. E05202200048) and Wagon Repair Shop, Raipur (Estt. No. E11152200001). A grand total of 1644 vacancies (1363 in Raipur Division + 281 in Wagon Repair Shop) have been announced across multiple designated trades. Online applications are accepted exclusively through the National Apprenticeship Training Portal at https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in from 05.05.2026 to 04.06.2026 (till 23:59 hrs). No physical copy of the application is required to be sent to the office. Candidates are advised to check their eligibility carefully before applying to avoid disappointment at a later stage.

  • Download SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Notification PDF

SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Vacancy 2026-27

A total of 1644 vacancies have been notified across two establishments for multiple designated ITI trades. The vacancies are distributed across reservation categories - UR (Unreserved), EWS (Economically Weaker Section), OBC (Other Backward Classes), SC (Scheduled Caste), and ST (Scheduled Tribe).

Horizontal reservations are also provided for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) and Ex-Servicemen (Ex.SM). The Fitter and Electrician trades have the highest number of vacancies in the Raipur Division, while Fitter and Welder dominate the Wagon Repair Shop vacancies. Candidates may note that if EWS category seats remain unfilled, they will be filled as per SECR/HQ clarification No. P-HQ/RUL/101/10 (E-50910) dated 13.02.2024.

SECR Raipur Division Act Apprentice Eligibility Criteria 2026-27

Candidates must fulfil both the educational/technical qualification requirements and the age limit to be eligible for this recruitment. The educational qualifications are mandatory and must have already been obtained before the date of notification - candidates appearing in qualifying examinations or awaiting results are not eligible.

The minimum 10th class marks of 50% aggregate is mandatory and the fraction will be applied strictly (e.g., 49.9% will be treated as less than 50%).

ITI qualification must be in the relevant trade from a recognised institution. Candidates are not required to wait for their ITI marksheet if already passed - they must have the actual pass certificate in hand at the time of application. Council Registration (NCVT/SCVT) of the ITI is also required to be verified during document verification.
